message:
feat(tui): expand TUI with interactive navigation and view switching (+110 lines)

Extend TUI rendering with interactive navigation, view switching, and
pages_wizard improvements. Rotate stale beads history and sync tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -95,7 +95,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9.2","title":"Preserve explainability while adding responsive visibility rules and drill paths to the rebuilt insights console","description":"Purpose:
Ensure the redesigned insights screen remains trustworthy and explorable, not just visually denser.

Required scope:
1. Preserve or improve the existing explanation and proof affordances.
2. Add responsive visibility rules so important modules survive small screens while lower-value detail degrades gracefully.
3. Add drill paths from at-a-glance insight modules into deeper detail or adjacent workflows.

Acceptance:
1. Insights mode remains explainable and navigable while becoming denser and more visual.
2. Narrow and tiny layouts still preserve the most important insight modules.
3. Unit/state and journey tests cover explainability and drill workflows.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Explanations must stay close enough to the visual modules that users can trust what they are seeing without hunting for meaning.\n2. Drill paths should preserve context and avoid dumping users into unrelated screens without explanation.\n3. Responsive visibility rules must rank modules by operator value, not by implementation convenience.\n4. Low-data and partially computed states should remain transparent about what is missing or approximate.\n\nFuture-self note: A denser insights screen that users do not trust is worse than the current one. Explainability and provenance are part of the product, not add-ons.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Insights mode remains explainable and navigable while becoming denser and more visual.\n2. Narrow and tiny layouts still preserve the most important insight modules and clearly communicate omitted or approximate detail.\n3. Drill paths preserve user context and make sense from the originating module.\n4. Unit/state, journey, and scenario-driven proof cover explainability, drill workflows, and low-data/partial-compute states with detailed logging.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.000135865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:25:55.953644186Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["explainability","frankentui","insights","responsive","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:55.000135865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4","title":"Audit and close export/pages/workspace integration parity gaps","description":"Background:
Legacy `bv` is more than robot JSON and a TUI. It also includes export hooks, workspace-aware behavior, pages export and preview flows, and operator-facing deployment workflows that can quietly diverge when path semantics or bundle contracts differ.

Goal:
Rebuild confidence in the integration surfaces by auditing and then closing gaps in static export artifacts, preview/watch behavior, pages wizard flows, workspace aggregation, and hook execution semantics.

Acceptance:
- Export artifacts and runtime bundle semantics are explicitly checked against legacy expectations.
- Preview/watch/pages workflows are validated for both happy-path and failure-path behavior.
- Workspace and repo-path semantics are proven consistent across integration surfaces.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.154964889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.149035060Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","pages","parity","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.154964889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":130,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Integration track guidance:
Treat path-resolution semantics as a recurring root-cause category. Recent bugs in hooks, feedback storage, preview serving, and wizard repair flow all came from seemingly minor cwd/repo/workspace assumptions. Audit those assumptions first, then fix bundle/runtime behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","title":"Re-audit static export bundle semantics and viewer-runtime contracts","description":"Purpose:
Audit the exported bundle as a product artifact: asset inventory, bootstrap payloads, offline/static-host assumptions, CSP/cache behavior, SQLite or JSON sidecars, and viewer startup expectations.

Why this matters:
A pages export that exists but does not behave like the legacy artifact is not real parity.

Done means:
We have a concrete list of matching and missing bundle semantics, plus any required fix tasks.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.937867751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T21:58:55.942860334Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T21:58:55.698935661Z","close_reason":"Completed export/runtime contract audit slice: landed shared triage payload parity for project_health and quick_ref counters, updated contract test to reflect must-have-now status, and left history timeline as the documented deferred gap.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bundle","export","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:32.256517849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.937867751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":159,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","author":"WhiteMink","text":"Using bv robot triage to pick the next feasible slice. Current top pick is bd-2bpl.3.2, but src/tui.rs is already actively dirty, so I am taking bd-2bpl.4.1 as the highest-impact export/runtime bead I can move without collision. I am auditing static bundle contracts in src/export_pages.rs, tests/export_pages.rs, and viewer assets, and I will land concrete export-contract fixes instead of just notes.","created_at":"2026-03-15T21:51:52Z"},{"id":160,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","author":"WhiteMink","text":"Audit/update complete: triage payload now exports project_health plus legacy quick_ref counters (open_count/actionable_count/blocked_count/in_progress_count), so both --robot-triage and pages export share the richer contract the viewer/schema already expected. I updated the export contract matrix to mark those two items must-have-now and verified with rch via cargo test --lib triage_, cargo test --test export_pages export_pages_contract_makes_current_parity_status_explicit -- --exact, cargo check --all-targets, and cargo clippy --all-targets. Remaining explicit deferred gap in this lane is the richer history timeline schema, which is still documented in tests/export_pages.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-15T21:58:55Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","title":"Re-audit preview/watch/pages wizard workflows including failure-path operator experience","description":"Purpose:
Audit the end-to-end pages workflow: export, preview, live reload, watch loops, deploy-target configuration, saved config repair, and wizard transcripts or operator guidance. Failure paths matter as much as happy paths.

Acceptance:
The bead identifies where the workflow is equivalent, where it is weaker, and which failures still confuse or strand the operator.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.282086233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T04:29:06.190338711Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pages","parity","preview","watch","wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:32.925253621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.282086233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":168,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Taking the next feasible high-impact slice from bv robot triage. I’m auditing preview/watch/pages behavior in src/export_pages.rs and tests/export_pages.rs for another concrete operator-visible failure-path or path-semantics bug.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:09:49Z"},{"id":169,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Found and fixed another preview/pages parity gap in src/export_pages.rs. Problem: the local preview server returned 404 for directory routes without a trailing slash (for example /docs) even when docs/index.html existed, which is weaker than normal static-host behavior and can strand operators when testing nested exported routes. Fix: resolve_preview_asset_path() now serves directory index files when the canonicalized target is a directory containing index.html. Added regression coverage in tests/export_pages.rs: preview_pages_serves_directory_index_without_trailing_slash. Verification via rch: cargo test --test export_pages preview_pages_serves_directory_index_without_trailing_slash -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check was not rerun in this slice; repo still has unrelated pre-existing formatting drift elsewhere.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:11:21Z"},{"id":173,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Continuing bd-2bpl.4.2 for another concrete pages/preview/watch slice since the higher-ranked TUI and robot lanes remain active elsewhere. Next pass is focused on preview/watch/pages-wizard behavior in src/export_pages.rs, src/pages_wizard.rs, and focused pages tests.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:20:27Z"},{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Verified another concrete pages-wizard parity fix in src/pages_wizard.rs. Problem: when a saved config was missing output_path, the wizard’s repair path sent the operator to Target Settings instead of Export Options, which is the wrong recovery step and can strand the user in the wrong prompt flow. Fix: repair_step_for_saved_config() now returns ExportOptions when output_path is missing before considering deploy_target. Updated the interactive regression wizard_interactive_saved_local_config_missing_output_reprompts_export_options to drive the repaired Export Options -> Deploy Target -> Local Target Config path explicitly. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib pages_wizard::tests::wizard_interactive_saved_local_config_missing_output_reprompts_export_options -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check still fails from unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:26:12Z"},{"id":177,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Found and fixed another concrete preview failure-path mismatch in src/export_pages.rs. Problem: the preview server still served /.bvr/livereload even when --no-live-reload was enabled, which contradicted the advertised disabled status and meant the reload endpoint was not actually disabled. Fix: handle_preview_request() now returns 404 for the reload endpoint when live_reload is false. Added regression coverage in tests/export_pages.rs: preview_pages_no_live_reload_disables_reload_endpoint. Verification via rch: cargo test --test export_pages preview_pages_no_live_reload_disables_reload_endpoint -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check still fails from unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in many other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:29:06Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","title":"Re-audit preview/watch/pages wizard workflows including failure-path operator experience","description":"Purpose:
Audit the end-to-end pages workflow: export, preview, live reload, watch loops, deploy-target configuration, saved config repair, and wizard transcripts or operator guidance. Failure paths matter as much as happy paths.

Acceptance:
The bead identifies where the workflow is equivalent, where it is weaker, and which failures still confuse or strand the operator.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.282086233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:14.072405419Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pages","parity","preview","watch","wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:32.925253621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.282086233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":168,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Taking the next feasible high-impact slice from bv robot triage. I’m auditing preview/watch/pages behavior in src/export_pages.rs and tests/export_pages.rs for another concrete operator-visible failure-path or path-semantics bug.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:09:49Z"},{"id":169,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Found and fixed another preview/pages parity gap in src/export_pages.rs. Problem: the local preview server returned 404 for directory routes without a trailing slash (for example /docs) even when docs/index.html existed, which is weaker than normal static-host behavior and can strand operators when testing nested exported routes. Fix: resolve_preview_asset_path() now serves directory index files when the canonicalized target is a directory containing index.html. Added regression coverage in tests/export_pages.rs: preview_pages_serves_directory_index_without_trailing_slash. Verification via rch: cargo test --test export_pages preview_pages_serves_directory_index_without_trailing_slash -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check was not rerun in this slice; repo still has unrelated pre-existing formatting drift elsewhere.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:11:21Z"},{"id":173,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Continuing bd-2bpl.4.2 for another concrete pages/preview/watch slice since the higher-ranked TUI and robot lanes remain active elsewhere. Next pass is focused on preview/watch/pages-wizard behavior in src/export_pages.rs, src/pages_wizard.rs, and focused pages tests.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:20:27Z"},{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Verified another concrete pages-wizard parity fix in src/pages_wizard.rs. Problem: when a saved config was missing output_path, the wizard’s repair path sent the operator to Target Settings instead of Export Options, which is the wrong recovery step and can strand the user in the wrong prompt flow. Fix: repair_step_for_saved_config() now returns ExportOptions when output_path is missing before considering deploy_target. Updated the interactive regression wizard_interactive_saved_local_config_missing_output_reprompts_export_options to drive the repaired Export Options -> Deploy Target -> Local Target Config path explicitly. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib pages_wizard::tests::wizard_interactive_saved_local_config_missing_output_reprompts_export_options -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check still fails from unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:26:12Z"},{"id":177,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Found and fixed another concrete preview failure-path mismatch in src/export_pages.rs. Problem: the preview server still served /.bvr/livereload even when --no-live-reload was enabled, which contradicted the advertised disabled status and meant the reload endpoint was not actually disabled. Fix: handle_preview_request() now returns 404 for the reload endpoint when live_reload is false. Added regression coverage in tests/export_pages.rs: preview_pages_no_live_reload_disables_reload_endpoint. Verification via rch: cargo test --test export_pages preview_pages_no_live_reload_disables_reload_endpoint -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check still fails from unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in many other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:29:06Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"CobaltSpring","text":"Audit slice update (CobaltSpring): found and fixed a concrete pages-wizard failure-path bug in src/pages_wizard.rs. Problem: malformed saved configs with an empty output_path ("") were treated as valid because validation only checked whether output_path was None, so the repair flow could skip Export Options instead of prompting the operator to choose a real bundle path. Fix: added WizardConfig::has_output_path() and routed both validate_for_export() and repair_step_for_saved_config() through it so empty PathBuf values are treated as missing output. Added focused regressions for direct validation and the interactive saved-config repair path. Verification via rch: cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo clippy --all-targets passed with only the repo's existing warning backlog; cargo fmt --check remains red repo-wide on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice. Note: there is unrelated worktree drift in src/tui.rs that I did not touch.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","title":"Re-audit workspace, hook, and repo-path semantics across all integration surfaces","description":"Purpose:
Many recently fixed bugs came from path-resolution assumptions. This audit must look for the same class of issue across exports, hooks, feedback/config files, workspace loading, watch paths, preview serving, and related operational flows.

Done means:
Path semantics are treated as a first-class parity surface with explicit evidence and follow-up tasks.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.639260766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T04:01:15.040775744Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hooks","parity","paths","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:33.596122596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.639260766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":145,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"BrightSpire","text":"Audited baseline/drift path semantics and fixed a real workspace-root mismatch.

Problem: --baseline-info, --save-baseline, --check-drift, and --robot-drift were still using raw --repo-path (or .) instead of the resolved load target/project directory. When --repo-path pointed inside a repo that should auto-promote to a workspace root via .bv/workspace.yaml, baseline files were read/written under the nested repo instead of the workspace root.

Fixes landed:
- src/main.rs now routes all four baseline/drift entry points through project_dir_for_load_target(&cli)
- workspace_discovery_start_points() now normalizes relative --repo-path values against current_dir before dedup/discovery, preventing false ambiguity between relative and absolute views of the same workspace root

Evidence/tests:
- added unit test: resolve_issue_load_target_discovers_workspace_from_relative_repo_path_without_ambiguity
- added integration test: baseline_commands_use_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace
- verification passed via rch: cargo test --test cli_model_validation baseline_commands_use_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets
- cargo fmt --check still fails repo-wide on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice; touched files were rustfmt-formatted locally","created_at":"2026-03-15T03:28:39Z"},{"id":161,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"WhiteMink","text":"Taking a concrete follow-up under the workspace/path audit: --robot-orphans currently reloads git commits from raw --repo-path / . even though the shared history path already resolves repo root via project_dir_for_load_target() + resolve_repo_root(). In workspace auto-discovery mode that can narrow orphan analysis to the nested repo while issue/history loading is workspace-aware. I am patching that mismatch and adding a nested --repo-path workspace regression.","created_at":"2026-03-16T03:38:40Z"},{"id":162,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"WhiteMink","text":"Landed another concrete workspace/path parity fix in src/main.rs: --robot-orphans now reloads git commits via resolve_repo_root(&cli) instead of raw --repo-path / ., so nested --repo-path invocation that auto-discovers a workspace no longer narrows orphan analysis to the nested repo. Added integration coverage in tests/cli_model_validation.rs for nested repo-path workspace discovery plus workspace-root git history. Manual command-level sanity check shows total_commits=2 and candidate_files includes apps/web/src/orphan.js in that scenario. Standard rch cargo test/check attempts were intermittently blocked by worker /tmp ENOSPC during C dependency compilation; cargo clippy --all-targets did complete on another worker with the repo’s existing warning-only output.","created_at":"2026-03-16T03:43:42Z"},{"id":164,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added another workspace/path fix in src/main.rs: relative history/snapshot references now prefer the resolved project/workspace root before caller cwd shadow files. This closes a class of `--diff-since snapshots/...` bugs when invoking from outside the repo with --repo-path pointing into a workspace-discovered project. Added integration coverage in tests/cli_model_validation.rs for a caller-cwd shadow snapshot versus the intended workspace snapshot. Verification: rch cargo clippy --all-targets passed on vmi1264463 with the repo's existing warning-only output; rch cargo test --test cli_model_validation robot_diff_snapshot_prefers_workspace_relative_file_over_caller_cwd_shadow -- --exact and rch cargo check --all-targets are still active behind a worker-local build lock, so those two are not yet confirmed complete.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:01:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","title":"Implement the highest-value integration fixes found by export/pages/workspace audit","description":"Purpose:
Implementation bucket for concrete export/pages/workspace mismatches uncovered by the fresh audit. Use this bead to track real code fixes, not speculative improvements.

Acceptance:
Each landed fix includes targeted regression tests and preserves deterministic operator behavior under explicit `--repo-path`, workspace-root, and non-repo cwd invocation scenarios.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.990920814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.702204761Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","implementation","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.990920814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:34.305098979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:35.087430954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:35.773187319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","title":"Fix static export bundle and viewer-runtime mismatches surfaced by the integration audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes from `bd-2bpl.4.1` for export artifact parity: asset inventory, bootstrap/runtime payloads, offline/static-host assumptions, CSP/cache semantics, and viewer startup behavior.

Acceptance:
- Exported bundles behave correctly in the environments legacy users rely on.
- No existing export capability is removed or weakened.
- Root causes and proof obligations are recorded clearly.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:15.353993659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:36.551075321Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bundle","export","implementation","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:36.550288954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:15.353993659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
